_meta.purpose: Hidden knowledge for NPC only — NOT given to main LLM

verbal_knowledge.answer: When I retired in August, I took a partial capital withdrawal from my Pensionskasse — CHF 250,000 as a one-time lump sum. This is documented in the Pensionskasse confirmation letter. It's taxed separately from regular income.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: income.capitalwithdrawals.amount = CHF 250,000. This must be declared on the separate Kapitalbezüge form, taxed at reduced rate. This is often missed by the LLM.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I retired on August 31, 2025, after 35 years at Zurich Insurance. The pension payments started on September 1st. So I was employed for 8 months (January to August) and received pension for 4 months (September to December).
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Employment income covers Jan-Aug 2025 only. BVG pension income = CHF 3,200 × 4 months = CHF 12,800. AHV pension starts separately.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I still have a Freizügigkeitskonto with CHF 180,000 that I haven't drawn down yet — it's at Stiftung Auffangeinrichtung BVG. It earns interest. Yes, I need to declare the balance as wealth and the interest as income.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Freizügigkeitskonto: balance CHF 180,000 = wealth. Interest CHF 900 = income. Declared separately from main bank account.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, Zurich Insurance had a subsidized canteen. I used it regularly during my employment there.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Thomas Lohnausweis field 14.1 = Ja → Thomas verpflegung = CHF 1,600 (prorated for 8 months? Actually per Lohnausweis for the period worked)

verbal_knowledge.answer: Elisabeth works at the Zentralbibliothek — they don't have a subsidized canteen there. She brings lunch from home.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Elisabeth Lohnausweis field 14.1 = Nein → Elisabeth verpflegung = CHF 3,200. Total = 1600 + 3200 = CHF 4,800.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We own our home in Küsnacht outright — the mortgage is completely paid off. We bought the house decades ago and paid off the last installment several years ago.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: No mortgage outstanding balance. No Schuldzinsen deduction. Property still has Eigenmietwert and Steuerwert from assessment.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We had quite a few medical bills this year — my hip operation and Elisabeth's dental work. Total came to about CHF 5,200. We have all the receipts. But our accountant friend mentioned the 5% threshold — I'm not sure if we clear it.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Medical costs CHF 5,200. Must exceed 5% of net income to be deductible. Given combined income ~CHF 198,000+, net income after deductions likely > CHF 100,000, so 5% threshold > CHF 5,000. May clear the threshold only marginally. Ground truth = CHF 0 deduction (below threshold).

verbal_knowledge.answer: We have a car — a BMW 3 series. We use it mainly for personal trips, not for commuting. Elisabeth cycles to the library.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Vehicle is for personal use, not commuting. No Fahrkosten from car.

verbal_knowledge.answer: We have a securities portfolio at UBS worth about CHF 420,000. We received about CHF 8,600 in dividends this year from Swiss and foreign companies.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Securities value = CHF 420,000 for wealth declaration. Dividends = CHF 8,600 income.

verbal_knowledge.answer: After I retired, I can no longer contribute to Pillar 3a. Elisabeth still works and contributes CHF 7,056 to her 3a account — the maximum for someone with BVG coverage.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Only Elisabeth's 3a = CHF 7,056 can be deducted. Thomas retired Aug 2025 and cannot make further 3a contributions.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I receive AHV pension of CHF 2,390 per month, starting from September. For 4 months that's CHF 9,560 in 2025. The Rentenbescheinigung document shows this.
Elisabeth still works full-time as a librarian at the Zentralbibliothek. Her Lohnausweis shows her full-year income.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Elisabeth's income is from her Lohnausweis — full year employment.

clarifications.answer: I withdrew CHF 250,000 from my Pensionskasse when I retired. It's a one-time lump sum payment documented in the Pensionskasse letter. This is taxed separately — I believe it goes on a separate form for Kapitalleistungen.
clarifications.tax_relevance: income.capitalwithdrawals.amount = CHF 250,000. Shown in pensionskasse_thomas.txt. This is the most commonly missed field.

clarifications.answer: For the professional expense deduction — Thomas worked 8 months and Elisabeth works full-time. You need to calculate 3% of each person's net salary separately and then add them together. The minimum is CHF 2,000 per person.

We have two accounts to declare: our main UBS savings account, and my Freizügigkeitskonto at Stiftung Auffangeinrichtung BVG with CHF 180,000. Both need to be listed with their year-end balances and interest earned.
clarifications.tax_relevance: Two bank accounts: main UBS (see bank_statement.csv for balance and interest) and Freizügigkeitskonto CHF 180,000 (interest CHF 900 from freizuegigkeitskonto_thomas.txt)
